goal: A GENUINE long-horizon autonomous run: dispatch and supervise a REAL live GRPO (RLVR) training run on a rented RunPod GPU to completion, measured end-to-end by the long-horizon harness's own monotonic clock. The dominant phase is real GPU compute (vLLM-colocate GRPO on a 9B model: dependency build, model download, GRPO training, held-out adapter-eval), NOT padding or a sleep loop. The harness times the whole pod lifecycle (rent -> SSH -> dep-install -> GRPO train -> adapter-eval -> copy-back -> delete pod); the SSIL Layer-1 gate then ingests the FRESH pod adapter-eval. Substantive by BOTH the duration tier (>=1800s real GPU compute) and the tool-call count. This is the honest answer to agi-proof/long-horizon-runs/HONEST-WALL-2026-06-27.md section 4: run the harness on the box that actually has the compute.
plan: provenance: record runner env, repo head, and the dispatch parameters
dry-run the RunPod payload (no pod, no cost) to sanity-check the request
LIVE: rent a GPU, run GRPO over SSH to completion, copy back the adapter-eval, delete the pod (the long real-compute phase)
ingest ONLY the FRESH pod adapter-eval into the SSIL Layer-1 gate (fail-closed; never re-gate a committed archive)
objective gate: a fresh pod adapter-eval report exists and parses (execution truth that a real GRPO run completed)

steps.name: provenance
cmd: python3 --version; echo head=$(git rev-parse HEAD); echo "params: task=$LH_TASK epochs=$LH_EPOCHS seed=$LH_SEED gpu='$LH_GPU' branch=$LH_BRANCH mode=$LH_MODE interruptible=$LH_INTERRUPTIBLE"
steps.purpose: record runner environment, repo head, and dispatch parameters

steps.name: dry-run-payload
steps.purpose: sanity-check the RunPod request payload without renting a GPU (no cost)

steps.name: live-grpo-runpod
cmd: INT=; [ "$LH_INTERRUPTIBLE" = true ] && INT=--interruptible; set -x; python3 tools/runpod_rlvr.py --yes --source git --branch "$LH_BRANCH" --remote-mode "$LH_MODE" --task "$LH_TASK" --epochs "$LH_EPOCHS" --seed "$LH_SEED" --quant bf16 --vllm colocate --gpu-type "$LH_GPU" $INT
steps.purpose: REAL long-running compute: rent GPU + GRPO over SSH + held-out adapter-eval + always delete the pod

steps.name: ssil-gate-ingest
cmd: found=$(ls -t agi-proof/benchmark-results/runpod-rlvr/*adapter-eval*.json 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [ -n "$found" ]; then echo "Ingesting FRESH pod report (newest mtime): $found"; python3 tools/ingest_rlvr_eval.py "$found" --print --hardened --registry agi-proof/benchmark-results/rlvr-replication/registry.jsonl; else echo 'No fresh adapter-eval copied back from the pod (fail-closed); not gating any committed archive.'; exit 1; fi
steps.purpose: gate ONLY the freshest pod adapter-eval (newest mtime = the one THIS run copied back) through the SSIL Layer-1 gate; ls -t avoids the non-deterministic find|head-1 ordering that can pick a stale committed eval. A gate REJECT is a legitimate scientific outcome, not a harness failure
